title: Open The Visu_MDs HMI Screen To Set Initial Tipper Controller Parameters
summary: Access the operator station HMI Machine Data screen (Visu_MDs) as the documented starting point for setting initial controller parameters for each tipper axis during tipper commissioning. The source supports the required login level and navigation to the screen, but does not provide the actual parameter entries or save/update actions for this specific step.
when_to_use: Use during tipper commissioning when initial controller parameters must be accessed for each tipper axis, particularly in the commissioning context described after tipper component replacement.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to enter or change specific parameter names or values, because they are not provided in the source excerpt.
Do not use this runbook to perform save, update, or confirmation actions for controller parameters, because those actions are not provided in the source excerpt.
Do not use this runbook as a complete tipper commissioning procedure; it only covers the source-backed access steps for reaching the Visu_MDs screen.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Log in with maintenance or admin access and navigate the operator station HMI to the Visu_MDs screen for controller parameter setup.

safety_notes: This procedure is part of tipper commissioning activity associated with replaced tipper components and changing controller settings.
You must be logged in as maintenance or admin to be able to change the settings.
The source does not provide enough detail to safely perform actual parameter changes within this runbook.

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
Maintenance or admin login on the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Tipper commissioning context applies when controller parameters need to be set for each tipper axis.

visual_references.description: Operator station HMI screen referenced during tipper commissioning for setting controller parameters.
Operator Station HMI Machine Data screen showing axis parameter settings for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.

steps.title: Log in with maintenance or admin access
steps.purpose: Ensure the user has the required access level to change settings on the operator station HMI.
steps.instruction: Log in to the operator station HMI using a maintenance or admin account before attempting to change controller settings.
steps.expected_result: The user is logged in with sufficient privileges to change settings.
steps.healthy_condition: A maintenance or admin session is active on the operator station HMI.
steps.failure_condition: Maintenance or admin access is unavailable or the user is not logged in at the required level.

actions.label: Log in to HMI
actions.exact_action: Log in to the operator station HMI with a maintenance or admin account to allow settings changes.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI screen referenced for controller parameter setup during tipper commissioning.
Login/logout controls and logged-in user ID area on the operator station HMI.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if maintenance or admin access is not available.
Stop and escalate if you cannot confirm the HMI session has sufficient privileges to change settings.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: NOTE: You must be logged in as maintenance or admin to be able to change the settings.
Changing settings in the controller parameter setup context requires a maintenance or admin login on the operator station HMI.

steps.title: Navigate to the Visu_MDs screen
steps.purpose: Open the documented HMI screen used as the starting point for controller parameter setup.
steps.instruction: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MDs" screen using F2.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MDs screen is displayed on the operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station HMI shows the Visu_MDs Machine Data screen.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MDs screen does not open or the user reaches a different screen.

actions.label: Open Visu_MDs
actions.exact_action: Navigate to the "Visu_MDs" screen using F2 on the operator station HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator station HMI screen referenced in the commissioning section for accessing Visu_MDs.
The Machine Data screen layout for axes Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if F2 does not open the Visu_MDs screen.
Stop and escalate if the displayed screen does not match the Machine Data screen used for axis parameters.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MDs" screen (F2).
VISU_MDS is the Operator Station HMI Machine Data screen. It contains parameters for axes Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 and includes a COPY FROM OPPOSITE button.

steps.title: Use Visu_MDs as the starting point for axis parameter setup
steps.purpose: Position the user at the documented screen where initial controller parameters for each tipper axis are accessed.
steps.instruction: Use the Visu_MDs screen as the documented starting point for setting the initial parameters for each axis of the tippers. Do not enter parameter names, values, or update actions from this runbook unless separately supported by source material.
steps.expected_result: The user is at the documented Machine Data screen used to access initial controller parameters for each tipper axis.
steps.healthy_condition: The Visu_MDs Machine Data screen is available and shows axis parameter areas for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
steps.failure_condition: The screen does not provide the expected axis parameter context, or the user attempts unsupported parameter entry steps not documented in this source excerpt.

actions.label: Confirm Machine Data screen context
actions.exact_action: Use the Visu_MDs screen as the documented starting point for setting the initial parameters for each tipper axis.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The commissioning-related operator station HMI screen used for controller parameter access.
Axis parameter sections for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 on the Machine Data screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the required parameter-setting details are needed but are not available in this source excerpt.
Stop and escalate if specific parameter names, values, or save/update actions are required to continue.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 7.3.23.2: SET CONTROLLER PARAMETERS - Set the initial parameters for each axis of the tippers.
The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2).

success_criteria: The user is logged in to the operator station HMI as maintenance or admin.
The Visu_MDs screen is opened using F2.
The Machine Data screen is available as the documented starting point for initial tipper axis controller parameter setup.
healthy_conditions: Maintenance or admin access is active on the operator station HMI.
The Visu_MDs Machine Data screen is displayed.
Axis parameter areas for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 are available on the screen.
failure_conditions: The user does not have maintenance or admin access.
The Visu_MDs screen cannot be reached using F2.
Specific parameter entry details are required but are not provided in this source excerpt.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if maintenance or admin access is not available to change settings.
Escalate if the HMI does not open the Visu_MDs screen as documented.
Escalate to SME/manual follow-up if specific parameter names, values, or save/update actions are needed, because they are not provided in this source-backed procedure.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station HMI screen referenced during tipper commissioning for setting controller parameters.
VISU_MDS Machine Data screen with axis parameters for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
Login/logout controls and logged-in user ID area relevant to confirming access level.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 7.3.23.2: SET CONTROLLER PARAMETERS - Set the initial parameters for each axis of the tippers.
On the operator station HMI, navigate to the "Visu_MDs" screen (F2).
NOTE: You must be logged in as maintenance or admin to be able to change the settings.
